title: Song - Persuasions To Enjoy
content:
If the quick spirits in your eye
    Now languish and anon must die;
    If every sweet and every grace
    Must fly from that forsaken face;
    Then, Celia, let us reap our joys
    Ere Time such goodly fruit destroys.

    Or if that golden fleece must grow
    For ever free from ag�d snow;
    If those bright suns must know no shade,
    Nor your fresh beauties ever fade;
    Then fear not, Celia, to bestow
    What, still being gather'd, still must grow.

    Thus either Time his sickle brings
    In vain, or else in vain his wings.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Carew, titled "Song - Persuasions To Enjoy", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
